name: Big Horn Federal Savings Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured federal savings bank serving the Big Horn Basin for over 85 years with consumer and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long: Big Horn Federal Savings Bank is a federally-insured financial institution that has operated in the Big Horn Basin region for more than 85 years. The bank positions itself as a community-focused provider committed to serving local banking needs with both traditional and modern digital solutions. The institution maintains FDIC insurance backing, meaning deposits are protected by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. On the consumer side, they provide checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loan products, and mortgage financing. Customers can access their accounts through free online internet banking, a mobile app, and phone-based telebanking. The bank also offers VISA debit cards, online bill payment, Zelle money transfers, and ATM access through the VISA network (750,000+ ATMs globally). Business customers have access to commercial checking, savings, and specialized commercial loan products. What distinguishes Big Horn Federal is its emphasis on personal service combined with digital accessibility. The bank highlights that all personal, digital, and contactless facilities remain open and operational. They're actively upgrading security infrastructure, implementing multi-factor authentication via email verification as of May 1st. The institution provides multiple channels for customer support including 24-hour ATM/debit card assistance, a dedicated telebanker line, online support, and local branch access. This omnichannel approach suggests commitment to serving customers across different preferences. Big Horn Federal is a straightforward community bank without the size or extensive product range of national megabanks. Their loan and rate offerings appear competitive but would require direct comparison. As a regional institution, geographic convenience may be limited unless you're in or near the Big Horn Basin area. The bank's website emphasizes reliability and personal service rather than premium rates or exotic products, positioning it as a dependable option for conventional banking needs.
services: Free online internet banking and bill payment
VISA debit card with PIN selection
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full U.S. Government faith and credit
Free online internet banking and mobile app with unlimited account access
Free online bill payment for U.S.-based bills with optional automatic recurring payments
VISA debit card with global ATM access (750,000+ VISA network ATMs plus 400,000+ financial institutions)
24-hour customer support for lost/stolen cards and emergency assistance via phone
Multiple contact channels including telebanking, online support, email, and local branches
Zelle integration for real-time money transfers
85+ years of established operations in community
Both consumer and business banking products under one institution
cons: Regional bank with limited geographic footprint—primarily serves Big Horn Basin area
Website does not display current interest rates for savings, checking, or CD products, requiring direct inquiry
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced financial planning products
Limited information about loan terms, APRs, or approval requirements on website
Security upgrade in May 2024 may create temporary inconvenience for existing security question users
best_for: Residents and businesses in the Big Horn Basin seeking full-service community banking
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits and established local bank relationships over premium rates
Individuals needing omnichannel access (branch, phone, online, mobile) with 24-hour support
Small to mid-sized businesses requiring commercial checking, savings, and business loan services
diagnosis: Big Horn Federal Savings Bank is best for individuals and businesses in the Big Horn Basin seeking stable, FDIC-insured banking with personal service and convenient digital access. The main caveat is that as a regional institution, it may not serve customers outside its geographic footprint, and current rates and detailed loan terms are not transparently posted online, requiring direct contact for product comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured federal savings bank serving the Big Horn Basin for 85+ years with consumer and business checking and loans.
